title: Representative Tool Call Examples
summary: Representative incident MCP calls include querying active incidents, fetching one incident, and adding a discussion note. These examples are intentionally labeled representative because no public official IcM MCP tool schema was verified.
analogy: The user says, 'Find the payments fire.' The agent fills out the dispatch form: service equals payments, severity equals Sev2, status equals active. The form, not the casual sentence, is what the backend receives.
diagram:
graph LR
NaturalLanguage[Natural language] --> ToolChoice[Tool choice]
ToolChoice --> Arguments[Validated arguments]
Arguments --> Result[Structured result]
Result --> Answer[Grounded answer]

drills.q: What transforms natural language into backend action?
drills.a: The agent selects an MCP tool and supplies schema-validated arguments.
add_discussion, because it writes to the incident record.
They model a transferable incident connector but are not verified official IcM MCP schemas.

teachItBack: A tool call is the precise bridge from user intent to backend operation. Read calls can answer questions; write calls must wait for approval and authorization. These JSON examples teach the pattern, not an official IcM contract.

glossary.definition: MCP JSON-RPC method used by a client to invoke a server tool.
Structured input values passed to a tool.
Machine-readable data returned by a tool.
